# Web3 Staking — Technical Reference & Staking Knowledge Base

> Web3 Staking is an independent, non-custodial dashboard for comparing wallet-based staking routes: how staking works, rewards, lock-ups, risks, and how to start and exit.

## Key Facts & Smart Contract Specs
- **Protocol Class**: Non-Custodial Decentralized Staking Infrastructure
- **Supported Chain IDs**: 1
- **Protocol Fee Structure**: 0.00% Markup Fee (Standard Network Gas Only)
- **Custody Architecture**: Non-custodial — assets delegated or staked via smart contracts; private keys remain in user self-custody
- **Security & Audits**: Open-source smart contracts audited by leading security firms; verified on block explorers
- **Practitioner Test Verification**: Tested & Verified by Practitioner (July 2026)

## How Web3 Staking Works

### 1. Asset Delegation & Staking
Connect a Web3 EVM wallet, select the desired asset or validator pool, authorize token allowance if required, and execute the staking transaction. Yield begins accumulating immediately.

### 2. Unstaking & Liquidity Redemption
Redeem staked assets or trade liquid receipt tokens (LSTs) directly on secondary markets without waiting for unbonding periods.

### 3. Essential Gas Fuel Warning
Maintain sufficient native gas tokens in your wallet to cover staking approvals, reward compounding, and unstaking claims.

## Safety, Audits & Anti-Phishing
- **Smart Contract Security**: Verified smart contract code published on block explorers.
- **Anti-Phishing Protocol**: Always verify domain authenticity and check contract approval targets prior to signing Web3 transactions.

## Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

### What fees are charged by Web3 Staking?
There is zero protocol fee (0.00%). Users pay standard network execution gas.

### Is Web3 Staking non-custodial?
Yes, you maintain 100% control of your private keys and assets at all times.
